items.title: Doctors issue warning over reemerging 'Benadryl Challenge' that's sending kids to the hospital
items.description: The trend has been linked to the deaths of at least two teenagers.

items.title: Fire department, Hazmat team respond to fire at Spartanburg County warehouse
items.description: SPARTANBURG COUNTY, S.C. (WSPA) - Multiple fire departments and Spartanburg County hazmat units are working to contain a fire at a warehouse storage facility. The North Spartanburg Fire District confirmed it and several other departments were called to the scene Sunday morning. The fire is contained and firefighters are working to extinguish it. The cause [...]

items.title: Southwest eases policy for plus-size passengers after backlash
items.description: Southwest changed two major policies when the calendar flipped to 2026. Now one is being pulled back.

items.title: SC child porn prosecutions: plea deals, dismissals dominate QCN analysis
items.description: The analysis revealed a prosecution record driven by plea deals that led to most 60% of the charges filed being dismissed.

items.title: Experimental pill promises new hope for deadly pancreatic cancer
items.description: The American Cancer Society estimates about 67,000 new cases of pancreatic cancer will be diagnosed in the U.S. this year and more than 52,000 people will die from the disease.

items.title: We're having the worst wheat crop in decades. You'll notice the ripple effects soon at the grocery store
items.description: It's a perfect storm of terrible conditions for wheat farmers this year. We may all pay the price.

items.title: Dry and cool Sunday ahead
items.description: Sunday will be mostly dry and also mostly cloudy. Some sunshine will be possible, mostly in the mountains. Temperatures will be much cooler with highs in the low to mid 70s. Another cold front arrives Monday increasing rain chances again. Some rain may linger into Tuesday, especially in the mountains. From there, dry and cooler [...]

items.title: Heavy rains threaten to delay search for 2 people still missing in Laos cave
items.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Heavy rains threatened to delay the search for two people missing in a flooded cave in Laos on Sunday, after the rescue of five other people who were trapped underground for over a week. Finnish diver Mikko Paasi, one of the first international rescuers to arrive at the site, told The Associated [...]

items.title: Bus driver in deadly Stafford crash taken into custody, charged with involuntary manslaughter
items.description: The driver of the charter bus that struck at least eight vehicles in Stafford County on Friday, killing five and injuring 44, has been taken into custody.

items.title: Redistricting battle set to escalate ahead of 2028 elections
items.description: For Americans fed up with partisan redistricting, there’s bad news on the horizon: The gerrymandering war is just heating up. Even while the ink is still drying on the new House maps of the midterm cycle, the 2028 redistricting battle is already taking shape — and it threatens to eclipse the current map-drawing fever in [...]

items.title: Pennsylvania mayor rails against police officer he says left for 'Love Island USA'
items.description: "... reality show participation wins out over being a police officer."

items.title: Thunder to be shorthanded again for Game 7 against Spurs in West finals
items.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — Jalen Williams and Ajay Mitchell will not play for the Oklahoma City Thunder in Game 7 of the Western Conference finals against the San Antonio Spurs on Saturday night because of injuries that have dogged both throughout the series. Williams has been dealing with a strained left hamstring for much of [...]

items.title: Man dies days after Oconee County crash
items.description: OCONEE COUNTY, S.C. (WSPA) - The Oconee County Coroner's Office said a man has died days after a crash in Seneca. Officials said a man had sustained injuries from a crash involving two cars in Seneca on Wednesday, May 27 on Bypass 123 near North Walnut Street. On Saturday at 4:50 p.m., the victim was [...]

items.title: 'I'm just extremely thankful to be here': Memorial day weekend boat explosion injures NC man
items.description: A Garner man is spending another night at the UNC burn center after a freak accident over Memorial Day weekend.

items.title: Trump plans to appeal order allowing all importers that paid struck-down tariffs to seek refunds
items.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Businesses big and small have started receiving tariff refunds after the U.S. Supreme Court ruled that President Donald Trump lacked the constitutional authority to impose higher import taxes on goods from nearly every other country. The process could grind to a halt, however, after the Trump administration said Friday that it [...]

items.title: Couple accused of using fake deed to steal home from niece
items.description: A niece says her uncle tried stealing the home she inherited from her grandmother.

items.title: Dog owner in deadly NC attack charged with involuntary manslaughter: Sheriff
items.description: The owner of the dogs involved in a deadly attack on a woman in Chatham County last week is facing a felony charge, deputies said Wednesday.

items.title: Trump plans ‘major speech’ after entertainers pull out of America 250 celebration
items.description: Trump said he was 'bigger than Elvis' in a Truth Social post.

items.title: 'Jaleeyah's Law': North Carolina bill named after slain teen seeks to enhance gang suppression laws
items.description: A bill named after a 13-year-old Goldsboro girl who prosecutors say was killed by teenagers with gang ties would strengthen North Carolina's gang suppression laws and create a specialized team dedicated to prosecuting gang-related crimes.

items.title: Wanted man accused of killing deputy in shootout on US 52 near North Carolina border
items.description: CARROLL COUNTY, Va. (WGHP) — The Carroll County Sheriff's Office is searching for a wanted man accused of killing a deputy and shooting another near the North Carolina border on Friday night. At around 9:26 p.m. on Friday, a pair of Carroll County deputies went to do a welfare check on 13658 Fancy Gap Highway/US [...]

items.title: McCormick grads celebrate at Emerald High School after fire damages gym
items.description: GREENWOOD, S.C. (WJBF) - One week after McCormick High School was hit by lightning and caught fire, graduates walked across the stage and got their diplomas at a different school. McCormick County High School's class of 2026 didn't have the end to their senior year they likely imagined. Because of the damage to the gym [...]

items.title: Rising Gas Prices Add $706 to the Cost of Driving a Gas-Powered Car
items.description: (iSeeCars) - The price of gasoline rose by nearly 46% between January 1 and April 30, 2026. But rising fuel prices are not affecting all vehicles equally. Annual fuel costs for gas-powered (non-hybrid) vehicles rose by $706 between January and April 2026, while the cost to drive EVs increased by just $11. The biggest financial [...]

items.title: Inflation gauge hits multiyear high as American consumer confidence slides
items.description: (AP) - The economy, inflation and how those forces could impact the lives of Americans were front and center over the past week. Trips to the grocery store or gas station are more painful than they were last year, and rising costs are impacting the decisions of both households and businesses. Here’s a snapshot of [...]

items.title: Pair of minor earthquakes reported in Upstate
items.description: SPARTANBURG COUNTY, S.C. (WSPA) - Instruments recorded two minor earthquakes in the Upstate on Friday. According to the US Geological Survey, a 1.8 magnitude earthquake was recorded shortly after 5 p.m. Friday approximately 8 kilometers east of Cross Anchor, in the Cross Keys area near S.C. Highway 49. Instruments detected the earthquake at a depth [...]

items.title: ICE agent charged in Minnesota shooting arrested in Texas
items.description: An arrest warrant was issued last week for the Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agent, who was placed on leave in February.
